chunks.text:
Joint statement: Working with other regulators to protect children online
Joint statement: Working with other regulators to protect children online
Online safety
Protecting children
Information for industry
Published:
24 March 2023
Last updated:
12 October 2023
This joint statement publicly announces the International Working Group on Age Verification, of which Ofcom is a founding member alongside
Autorité de régulation de la communication audiovisuelle et numérique (Arcom)
, the
Conseil Superieur de l’Audiovisuel (CSA)
, the
Cyprus Radiotelevision Authority (CRTA)
and
Direktorenkonferenz der Landesmedienanstalten (DLM)
.
The International Working Group has been established to help the respective members ensure that video-sharing platforms (VSPs) under their jurisdiction that have videos containing restricted material take their obligation to protect under-18s seriously, and implement robust access control measures to protect them from accessing harmful content on their services.
We recognise that online platforms are global in nature and can operate across multiple jurisdictions. This statement sets out details of our shared commitment to meeting the challenges of regulation in the 21
st
century.
Read the full statement
International Working Group's joint statement on age verification (PDF, 121.1 KB)
Enforcement programme into age assurance measures on UK-established, adult video-sharing platforms
This enforcement programme will look at whether UK-established, adult video-sharing platforms have appropriate age assurance measures in place, to protect children from pornographic material.
Video-sharing platform users' experiences and attitudes
Ofcom has commissioned research to understand people's experiences of using, and attitudes towards using video-sharing platforms (VSP).
Call for evidence: Second phase of online safety regulation
We will be publishing codes of practice setting out the steps platforms can take to protect children online as well as guidance on how platforms should assess risks of harms to children.
